title: Try to make a list
why.unchanged: You did not touch the code. Try — that is the whole lesson. You will fail, and that is exactly why you will need the next one. Have a go with dashes, with spaces, with whatever comes to mind.
blocks.0.text: The bike listing has to spell out what is included in the price. Four things: frame, front wheel, rear wheel, bell.
blocks.1.text:
You know paragraphs and you know
. So you can do this. Do it.
blocks.2.text: Try on your own before you read on
blocks.3.text: Put dashes in front. Spaces for indentation. Whatever comes to mind. Run it and look.
blocks.4.text: Does it work? By the look of it — yes. It looks like a list. It has bullets, it sits neatly.
blocks.5.text: Now try making one of the items a sub-item. Or number them and then reorder them. Or remove one from the middle. You will find every change is manual work — because this is not a list, it is text that looks like a list.
blocks.7.text: Your code says: "here is a paragraph with dashes and line breaks". It does not say "here are four things of the same kind".
blocks.8.text: Google does not know it is a list. The screen reader will not say "list of four items". You cannot renumber them if you reorder them. And every dash you typed by hand.
blocks.9.text: You drew a list. You did not make a list. The next lesson is about the difference.
